description: §22.11 / §22.12 E-META-001 (condition 3) — a JS-host ambient global (`console`) referenced inside a `^{}` meta body. Approach-C bans JS-host globals in any `^{}` body (compile-time OR runtime); `console` is not in the META_BUILTINS set, so it fires E-META-001. NOTE: the §22.11 condition-(1) trigger (runtime `@var` inside a compile-time `emit` block) is SHADOWED by E-META-005 phase-mixing — see meta-mixed-patterns-neg — so this case pins E-META-001 via the isolable condition-(3) JS-host-global path.
